mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in nonfarm payroll employment indicate a strengthening labor market, which boosts consumer income and spending, driving corporate earnings higher and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward analysis shows a Rank IC below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or the proportion of positive returns following PAYEMS increases is not significantly above 50% (binomial test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, monetary policy is perceived as overly restrictive, raising the discount rate on equity cash flows and increasing recession fears, which compresses valuation multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, the average 60-day SPX return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p>0.05), or the proportion of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and retail sales signal robust consumer demand and residential investment, indicating broad economic strength that supports corporate profits and drives positive equity returns over a one-month period.
failure_condition: The joint binary signal (both proxies rising month-over-month) does not result in positive returns in more than 55% of cases, or its information ratio is below 0.3 annually.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime (CPI level > 300), a decline in industrial production reveals supply-side constraints eroding corporate margins and economic output, raising stagflation probabilities and causing negative equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL > 300 and INDPRO has a negative month-over-month change, the 20-day forward SPX return is not negative in at least 60% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0% signals imminent labor market deterioration, which quickly undermines consumer confidence and spending expectations, leading to a sharp negative reaction in equity prices within a one-week window.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, the 5-day forward SPX return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p>0.05), or less than 55% of such events result in negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent expansion in industrial production reflects broad manufacturing growth, which leads corporate earnings and validates economic expansion, supporting positive equity returns over a longer three-month horizon as the trend becomes embedded in expectations.
failure_condition: The rolling Rank IC of INDPRO month-over-month change on 60-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year window, or the signal fails to produce a positive hit rate significantly above 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with rising unemployment (i.e., both CPIAUCSL and UNRATE increase month-over-month) creates a stagflationary impulse that damages both corporate margins and consumer purchasing power, leading to broad equity market declines over the next month.
failure_condition: When both series rise month-over-month, the 20-day forward SPX return is not negative in a majority of cases (≤50%) or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where the federal funds rate has risen by more than 25 basis points over the past three months, a decline in housing starts signals that the housing sector is contracting under tightening financial conditions, foreshadowing broader economic weakness and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met and HOUST declines month-over-month, the 20-day forward SPX return is not negative in at least 60% of instances, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05).
